structure_description: 13-section general market pricing + positioning overview for one country x industry. Front matter (cover / methodology / TOC) -> 'Pricing strategy' dark divider -> 2-page executive summary (snapshot + 5 pricing-action implications) -> tier stack -> indicative competitor price bands -> price-quality matrix (centerpiece) -> elasticity by segment -> WTP by consumer segment -> pricing models -> directional promo patterns -> premium-positioning case studies -> methodology endnote. Centerpiece is section 08 (price-quality matrix). Section 07 (indicative price bands) is supporting context, not a per-SKU pricing-committee table.

sections.purpose: Report cover — title, country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag. Title pattern lands on pricing: e.g. '{{country}} {{industry}} pricing strategy — {{year}}'.
sections.notes: No content generation needed — pure template fill from parsed topic metadata.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview of research approach — left col covers channel scans + tier-band observation; right col covers secondary sources (WTP surveys, regulator filings, listed-company disclosure) + KIRA estimate methods + source-tag conventions.
sections.notes: Voice: frame the method as a general market pricing read — 'Tier-level price observations sampled across modern trade, GT, and e-commerce, supplemented by secondary disclosure and survey data.' Do not over-promise per-SKU weekly scraping.

sections.purpose: Table of contents — generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Dark-mode chapter break — single thesis statement on the pricing inflection (e.g. 'Premium is pulling away. Mass is fragmenting.').
sections.notes: Mini-toc pills should preview the centerpieces: 'Tier stack', 'Price bands', 'Price-quality map', 'WTP by segment'.

sections.purpose: Two-page executive summary. Page 1: 4 callouts (dominant tier share, biggest pricing move 2024-2026, premium opportunity signal, prevailing promo direction) + 2-col narrative + tier-stack chart. Page 2: 5 strategic implications grid, each card framed as a pricing action — Hold / Discount / Premiumize / Fragment / Exit.
sections.char_budget_hint: callout num_max=12 chars, narrative_left total_chars_max=1200, implications cards body_max=360 chars each. Implication card tag_labels: 'HOLD', 'DISCOUNT', 'PREMIUMIZE', 'FRAGMENT', 'EXIT' (uppercase, <=18 chars).
sections.notes: Anchor each implication with a number or directional read from the body — premium share %, WTP gap, promo direction, tier momentum — not generic advice. Numbers can be ranges; precision is not required at the overview level.

chart_type_options.name: Tier stack — stacked share bar
chart_type_options.use_when: All four tiers (premium / mid / mass / value) have value-share data and the visual goal is composition at a single point in time

chart_type_options.name: Tier stack — ranked horizontal bars
chart_type_options.use_when: Tiers have value share AND a directional growth read, and the page 1 callouts already cover composition

chart_type_options.name: Tier share over 3 years
chart_type_options.use_when: Historical share by tier exists for 2023-2026 and the snapshot story is migration between tiers

sections.purpose: Four-tier stack chart (premium / mid / mass / value) with each tier's value share + volume share + 2-year directional growth. Narrative on left (3 sub-sections): where value is migrating, which tier is squeezed, where new entry is viable.
sections.notes: Chart is a stacked horizontal bar OR 4-row tier card stack. Tier definitions should be price-band anchored ('Premium = USD 12+ per unit') — band ranges are fine, point precision not required.

chart_type_options.name: Stacked horizontal bar (value + volume composition)
chart_type_options.use_when: Both value share and volume share are populated across the four tiers — primary recommended shape

chart_type_options.name: 4-row tier card stack
chart_type_options.use_when: Only value share is available, or tiers need a band-anchored label per row rather than a percentage split

chart_type_options.name: Value share vs 2-year growth dual axis
chart_type_options.use_when: Directional growth signal is strong enough to plot as a second series — emphasizes which tier is migrating

sections.purpose: 5-6 leading brands placed on a tier label + indicative price band (low-high range in local currency or USD) + qualitative directional change ('up', 'stable', 'down') over the prior 2 years. Sidebar narrative interprets the spread: who is drifting upmarket, who is holding the band, who is discounting into a lower tier.
sections.char_budget_hint: Table replaces the chart slot. 5-6 rows x 4 columns (brand, tier label, price band, directional change). Each cell value <= 22 chars. Sidebar narrative body_max=350 chars.
sections.notes: Supporting page, not a per-SKU pricing-committee table. Price bands are indicative ranges, not point prices; per-SKU pack-size detail is intentionally omitted. Directional change is qualitative ('up' / 'stable' / 'down'); no 2-year % change column. Source tags still required — most rows are [primary] (KIRA channel scan) or [secondary] (listed-company disclosure).

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 holds the top 5-6 brands in the table shape described above. Page 2 (id 07_competitor_price_bands_p2) extends the same table format to brands 7-10+, grouped by tier so the reader can scan within-tier spread; the sidebar narrative on p2 shifts from 'who is drifting' to 'within-tier dispersion and entry-band white space'.
expand_to_2_pages_if.fallback_to_1_page_if: brands_with_band_data < 10 — render the canonical 5-6 row table on one page

chart_type_options.name: Indicative price band bars (low-high range per brand)
chart_type_options.use_when: Brands have credible low-high band figures and the reader should see spread visually rather than as a table

chart_type_options.name: Brand price scatter on a single x-axis
chart_type_options.use_when: Bands compress to midpoints and the page can preview the §07 matrix lens — only when quality scoring is NOT yet ready (otherwise leave the matrix for §07)

sections.purpose: Perceptual map — 2x2 scatter of 6-12 brands placed on price (x-axis) x perceived quality (y-axis). Four quadrant labels framed for the category (e.g. 'Premium fortress' / 'Aspirational' / 'Mass-market core' / 'Value trap'). Sidebar narrative explains the structural insight — which quadrant is overcrowded, which has white space, who has migrated between quadrants.
sections.char_budget_hint: Matrix chart 380px tall. Brand labels <=18 chars each. Quadrant labels <=24 chars each. Sidebar narrative body_max=380 chars (2-3 sentences).
sections.notes: CENTERPIECE. Uses NEW page type 'price_quality_matrix' (shape sketched in manifest.yaml comment). Brand placement is qualitative — a tier-level band on the x-axis is acceptable. Y-axis quality score = KIRA composite (survey perception + functional attribute scoring) or single external survey index — disclose method in subhead. If category has >12 plottable brands, split into 2 matrix pages by segment and bump section_count to 14.

chart_type_options.name: Price-quality scatter (centerpiece)
chart_type_options.use_when: Always — this is the load-bearing shape for the price_quality_matrix page_type and the only option per A+ flex spec

sections.purpose: Directional read on price elasticity by consumer segment (urban / suburban / rural, OR mass / mid / aspirational, OR age cohort — pick category-appropriate cut). Chart: horizontal bar of elasticity coefficients with confidence ranges; OR table if coefficients come from disparate sources. Narrative explains where elasticity inverts (luxury inversion, status goods).
sections.skip_if: category has no published elasticity studies AND KIRA has no panel data — collapse into 06_tier_stack with elasticity as a narrative side-note
sections.char_budget_hint: Single chart with up to 6 segments. Confidence range as error bars. Narrative sections_count_max=3.
sections.notes: Elasticity coefficients are often [estimate] with a [secondary] anchor. Disclose the anchor explicitly in subhead — 'KIRA estimate calibrated to {{anchor_study}} 2024 [secondary]'. Qualitative ranges acceptable at the overview level.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 keeps the headline elasticity-by-segment chart and the 'where elasticity inverts' narrative. Page 2 (id 09_elasticity_p2) adds a second cut — e.g. category-occasion or price-tier-within-segment — plus a table of confidence ranges and anchor studies per cell, so the reader sees both the segment view and the conditional view without crowding either.
expand_to_2_pages_if.fallback_to_1_page_if: segments_with_elasticity_signal < 4 — render the canonical single-chart layout

chart_type_options.name: Elasticity coefficient bars with error whiskers
chart_type_options.use_when: Coefficients have comparable error ranges and a single cut (segment) is the right lens

chart_type_options.name: Elasticity vs current price index dual axis
chart_type_options.use_when: Showing the relationship between current price level and elasticity adds an inversion read

chart_type_options.name: Segment x occasion elasticity grid
chart_type_options.use_when: Elasticity varies meaningfully across both segment and purchase occasion and the page should expose the conditional view

sections.purpose: WTP bar chart with qualitative ranges per segment — what each segment will pay for the category benchmark vs current market price. Highlights the WTP gap (positive = pricing power untapped; negative = price exceeds WTP, churn risk).
sections.char_budget_hint: Bar chart with 4-6 segments. Each bar: WTP midpoint + range whiskers + current market price reference line. Narrative sections_count_max=3.
sections.notes: WTP data may be [primary] (KIRA panel), [secondary] (named credible survey, never aggregator), or [estimate] with anchor disclosed in subhead. Qualitative ranges acceptable — point precision is not required at the overview level.

chart_type_options.name: WTP bars vs current price reference line
chart_type_options.use_when: Each segment has a WTP midpoint + range and a single current-price reference is meaningful — default shape

chart_type_options.name: WTP vs current price dual axis
chart_type_options.use_when: Both WTP and current price should be shown as comparable series rather than bar + reference line

chart_type_options.name: WTP gap vs segment size bubble
chart_type_options.use_when: Segment size encoding matters — large gap on a small segment is a different signal than the same gap on a large segment

sections.title_pattern: Pricing models: subscription, one-time, freemium, tiered
sections.purpose: Table or matrix mapping pricing models (subscription / one-time / freemium / tiered / dynamic) to which consumer segments they work for, with adoption % and revenue contribution where data exists. Narrative covers which model is gaining share and why.
sections.skip_if: category has only one viable pricing model (commodity goods one-time only) — collapse insight into 06_tier_stack narrative
sections.char_budget_hint: Table with 4-5 model rows x 4 columns (segment fit / adoption % / typical price point / momentum). Sidebar narrative body_max=350 chars.
sections.notes: For services (telco, streaming, insurance) this is a load-bearing page. For pure consumer goods this section may collapse — orchestrator can skip if topic is shelf-good FMCG.

chart_type_options.name: Model-by-segment adoption share stacked bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Adoption % is populated across at least 3 of the 4-5 models and the page should show composition by segment

chart_type_options.name: Model x segment fit heatmap
chart_type_options.use_when: Fit is qualitative (rare / occasional / frequent / dominant) and crosses more than 3 segments — better than a table when the asymmetry is the story

chart_type_options.name: Revenue contribution by model
chart_type_options.use_when: Revenue split by model is the lead read (services categories like telco / streaming) and per-segment fit is secondary

sections.title_pattern: Promotional patterns by tier and channel
sections.purpose: Directional summary of promo patterns by tier x channel — e.g. 'premium tier rarely discounts (occasional gift-with-purchase only); mass tier flash-sale weekly on e-commerce; mid tier discounts seasonally in modern trade'. Visual: matrix or paired qualitative bars (rare / occasional / frequent) per tier-channel pair. Narrative covers channel asymmetry and tier-specific norms.
sections.skip_if: category has no promotional convention (utilities, regulated insurance) — skip and bump section_count to 12
sections.char_budget_hint: Matrix of 4 tiers x 4 channels with qualitative cells (rare / occasional / frequent / dominant). Narrative sections_count_max=3.
sections.notes: Directional read, not quantitative depth + frequency table. Source tags still required on the qualitative reads — [primary] for KIRA channel observation, [secondary] for trade-press promo coverage.

chart_type_options.name: Tier x channel qualitative heatmap
chart_type_options.use_when: Default shape — cells carry one of {rare / occasional / frequent / dominant} severity reads across tier x channel

chart_type_options.name: Promo share by channel within each tier
chart_type_options.use_when: Each tier has channel-mix data on promo intensity and the page wants to expose channel asymmetry as composition

sections.purpose: 2 brands that moved up successfully + 1 brand that tried and failed (if a credible failure case surfaces) — on a shared template page. Each case: brand, starting position, move attempted, outcome metric (share +/-, price realization +/-), one-line lesson. Format reuses competitive_profile_deep but compressed (2-3 cases on one page, not one case per page).
sections.skip_if: no clear premium-move case studies identified in research — collapse the lesson into 08_price_quality_matrix sidebar
sections.char_budget_hint: Reuse competitive_profile_deep shape but each case gets ~33-50% of the page. Per-case: name <=45 chars, summary body 300-380 chars, outcome stats 3-4 stat rows.
sections.notes: Failure case is optional. If a credible failure case surfaces in research, include it (premium-pricing coverage that only shows winners reads as marketing copy). If no failure case surfaces, ship with 2 successes — do not invent or stretch a weak example.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 holds the canonical 2 successes + 1 failure compressed layout. Page 2 (id 13_premium_case_studies_p2) adds 1-2 further cases — typically a contrasting geography or sub-segment — and a small comparison strip across all cases showing starting tier, move direction, and outcome metric so the reader can see the pattern, not just individual stories.
expand_to_2_pages_if.fallback_to_1_page_if: case_studies_available < 4 — render the canonical 2-success + 1-failure (or 2-success only) page

chart_type_options.name: Case comparison bubble — price move vs share move
chart_type_options.use_when: The expanded view (page 2) needs to compare 4+ cases on the same axes; size encodes pre-move revenue base

chart_type_options.name: Outcome metric bars per case
chart_type_options.use_when: Each case has a comparable outcome metric (share point change or price-realization change) and the page wants a single comparable read

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary]/[secondary]/[estimate] percentages — target 25/55/20 for this blueprint), channel-scan protocol disclosed (channels sampled, brand count, sampling window), WTP/elasticity estimate anchors disclosed, partial-data acknowledgement where coverage was thin, contact + next research footer.
sections.notes: Required — pricing overviews must show the method to earn trust, especially when the data is tier-band indicative rather than per-SKU exhaustive. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report.

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '012 / NN' in page-header counter slot — total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section (e.g. both exec_summary pages tagged 'Section 04 - Executive summary')
assembly_rules.divider_placement: Single dark divider at section 04 ('Pricing strategy'). No further dividers — this blueprint is shorter and tighter than market_analysis; extra dividers would inflate page count without adding signal.
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if condition triggers, omit the section AND recompute page counter for all subsequent sections. Floor: 11 sections (cover + methodology + toc + divider + exec_summary x2 + tier_stack + price_bands + matrix + methodology_endnote). Ceiling: 14 (if matrix splits into 2 segment maps).
assembly_rules.centerpiece_priority: Section 08 (price-quality matrix) is the load-bearing centerpiece. If section 07 (indicative price bands) can be populated with even 3-4 brands and qualitative direction, ship it; do not halt the pipeline for thin per-SKU coverage. Flag thin coverage in the methodology endnote instead.
